displayTitle: Plan the Shot Before the Source Moves

beats.narration: The levers haven't changed. You have. At Level Two you write the plan, and every dosimeter answers to it.

payload.title: OWN THE PLAN
payload.sub: Exposure control is a planning task now.

beats.narration: Activity decays. Certified at 100 curies one half-life ago, you hold 50 today: about 25 roentgen per hour at 1 meter.

payload.heading: Today's curies, not the certificate's
payload.note: ONE HALF-LIFE: 100 CI → 50 CI ≈ 25 R/H AT 1 M

beats.narration: Shot open, the restricted boundary sits near 110 meters. A collimator typically cuts the rate tenfold. Call it 35. The meter sets the rope.

payload.heading: Boundary at 2 mR/h: open vs collimated
payload.note: THE METER SETS THE ROPE — NOT THE MATH

beats.narration: Budget the shift, about eight millirem for twenty welds, then read dosimeters against it. Triple the estimate is a finding: investigate today.

payload.heading: Budget the shift before work starts

beats.narration: ALARA: as low as reasonably achievable. The annual occupational limit, five rem, is a ceiling, never an allowance. Plan it. Budget it. Question it.
